candidates.why: Separate same-level operating expense line, but not labelled as COGS/cost of sales depreciation; appears to be a broader operating D&A total rather than a COGS-specific amount.
Granular component under the broader D&A area; less appropriate than the summary row and not identified as COGS-specific.
Granular D&A component, not labelled as related to cost of goods/cost of sales.
Specific amortization component that looks more administrative/intangible-related than COGS-related.

variables.notes: The selected COGS bucket is only 'Cost of goods and change in inventory'. The excerpt shows no explicit D&A row identified as belonging to COGS/cost of sales. 'Depreciation expense' is a separate same-level operating line, but without COGS wording it should not be added to COGS_DA to avoid misallocation.

candidates.why: This is the clear summary D&A line in the operating section. It sits alongside, not beneath, the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows ('Marketing & Administrative Expenses', 'Employee benefits', 'Other Expenses'), and the operating expense total for the populated year equals Cost of goods + Employee benefits + Other Expenses + Depreciation expense, indicating it is separate rather than embedded in those selected rows.
Appears to be a component of the broader D&A disclosure. Lower priority than the summary row 'Depreciation expense', so should not be chosen if the summary is used.
Appears to be another D&A component beneath the broader D&A disclosure. Lower priority than the summary row to avoid double counting.
Looks like a specific amortization component, likely part of the broader D&A area; use only if no summary row existed.

variables.notes: A separate operating D&A summary row is visible above EBIT and is not contained within the selected XSGA_COMPONENTS rows. Therefore it should be added separately rather than assumed embedded. Manual review is warranted because the row is a total operating depreciation line without explicit SG&A-only wording, so some portion could theoretically relate outside SG&A; however, among the PROF buckets provided, it most plausibly belongs as a separate add-on to overhead/XSGA rather than to COGS, and the summary row is preferred over its components.
